sourceMovie.title: Dangerous Liaisons
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dangerous Liaisons works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Moulin Rouge!
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dangerous Liaisons clicked for you, Moulin Rouge! (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dangerous Liaisons?
faq.answer: Try Moulin Rouge!, Before Sunset, The Dreamers—each shares DNA with Dangerous Liaisons while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dangerous Liaisons?
faq.answer: Dangerous Liaisons (1988) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dangerous Liaisons worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
